commit.message:
Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add fly.toml for fly.io deployment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(email): migrate contact + newsletter routes from nodemailer to Resend

The nodemailer/SMTP routes were still in the main project directory —
only the worktree copies had been updated to Resend. This overwrites both
routes with the Resend HTTP-based implementation so they work on Vercel
serverless. Also wraps resend.emails.send() in try/catch to return a
proper JSON 500 instead of an unhandled exception.

commit.message:
docs: add MCP setup guides for Claude Code and Cursor, improve README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: Codex監査の残件6件を修正

1. High — Cross-Encoder データ流出防止:
   - リランカーへの送信をスニペット(200文字)に制限
   - 機密パターン(APIキー, トークン, 秘密鍵等)を自動マスキング

2. Medium — SSRF対策強化:
   - wildcard DNS (nip.io, sslip.io等) をブロック
   - IPv6-mapped IPv4, ユニークローカルアドレスをブロック
   - 127.0.0.0/8 全域をブロック（localhost のみ名前で許可）
   - http/https スキーマ制限を追加

3. Medium — memory_stats ACL完全化:
   - scope未指定時、ACL制限付きエージェントは自スコープのみ集計
   - 全体件数(totalEntries)の漏洩を防止

4. Medium — ツール入力サイズ制限:
   - memory_store/update: テキスト最大5000文字
   - memory_recall: クエリ最大1000文字
   - autoCapture: embed前にtruncate（全文でなくスニペットを埋め込み）
   - CLI --limit: NaN/不正値を拒否、上限1000

5. Low — dbPath検証をrelative()ベースに修正:
   - startsWith(home) → relative(home, resolved) で判定
   - /Users/sonia2/ のような prefix 一致の誤判定を防止

6. Low — 教訓抽出の TOCTOU 対策:
   - テキストハッシュによるインフライトセットで並行二重保存を防止
   - 30秒後に自動ロック解除（メモリリーク防止）

テスト: 87/87 パス

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve critical error handling gaps and add architecture docs

Fixes all critical High severity error handling gaps identified during
comprehensive codebase review and adds detailed architecture documentation.

## Backend Fixes (5 files)

### 1. backend/app/api/extraction.py
- Replace bare HTTPException with AppException system
- Check if exception is already AppException before re-raising
- Convert unknown exceptions to EXTRACTION_FAILED with proper context
- Preserves error codes and details through exception chain

### 2. backend/app/services/pubmed_fetcher.py
- Add null checks for XML .text attributes before string operations
- Protect author name parsing from None concatenation
- Add defensive checks in all XML parsing methods
- Change HTTPException to AppException in bulk_fetch_articles

### 3. backend/app/services/derived_properties_service.py
- Fix potential division by zero in consensus calculation
- Add explicit guard: `total if total > 0 else 0.0`

### 4. backend/app/services/document_service.py
- Add file size validation after reading content into memory
- Protect against memory exhaustion from large files
- Use AppException with DOCUMENT_TOO_LARGE error code
- Include file size context in error details

### 5. backend/app/mappers/entity_mapper.py
- Add type validation for JSON parsing results
- Handle unexpected data types gracefully
- Validate that parsed JSON is actually a dictionary
- Implement safe fallback chains for nested property access

## Frontend Fixes (5 files)

### 1. frontend/src/api/client.tsx
- Add NaN validation after parseInt in lock management
- Prevent arithmetic operations with NaN values
- Fix cross-tab token refresh edge cases

### 2. frontend/src/views/EditEntityView.tsx
- Fix syntax error: remove extra closing parenthesis
- Add missing error state variable declaration
- Ensure consistent error handling

### 3. frontend/src/views/CreateEntityView.tsx
- Add null checks before map operations on filterOptions
- Use optional chaining for safe property access
- Implement fallback chain: currentLanguage → en → id

### 4. frontend/src/notifications/NotificationContext.tsx
- Implement queue size limit (MAX_QUEUE_SIZE = 10)
- Drop oldest notifications when limit exceeded
- Prevent memory buildup during error storms

### 5. frontend/src/auth/AuthContext.tsx
- Differentiate authentication errors from network errors
- Only logout on actual auth failures (401, expired, etc.)
- Preserve session during transient network issues
- Improve user experience for offline scenarios

## Architecture Documentation

### New: docs/architecture/ERROR_HANDLING_ARCHITECTURE.md (900+ lines)

Comprehensive architecture documentation covering:

**1. Architecture Overview**
- Design principles and high-level architecture
- Component diagram and data flow

**2. System Components**
- Backend: Error classes, middleware, service patterns
- Frontend: Error parser, API client, notification system
- Error Details component for debugging

**3. Error Flow**
- Complete end-to-end error flow example
- Step-by-step tracing from frontend to backend and back

**4. Error Classification**
- 40+ error codes organized by category
- Authentication, validation, LLM, documents, database, etc.

**5. Implementation Details**
- Backend patterns: not found, validation, constraints, external APIs
- Frontend patterns: simple display, conditional handling, form validation
- Re-raising strategies and context enrichment

**6. Error Recovery Strategies**
- Automatic: token refresh, rate limit backoff (TODO)
- Manual: user retry, alternative paths, partial success

**7. Performance Considerations**
- Backend: response size, logging, database rollback
- Frontend: queue management, console logging, parsing optimization

**8. Security Considerations**
- Information disclosure prevention
- PII redaction, user enumeration prevention
- Error log access control

**9. Monitoring & Observability**
- Error metrics (TODO): rate tracking, distribution, user impact
- Structured logging strategy
- Log aggregation with Sentry/Datadog (TODO)

**10. Future Enhancements**
- Error analytics dashboard
- Automatic recovery with exponential backoff
- Error internationalization
- Error correlation and root cause analysis

## Impact

All critical High severity gaps resolved:
✅ No more bare HTTPException in backend
✅ All XML parsing protected against None values
✅ Division by zero prevented
✅ File uploads validated for memory safety
✅ Frontend NaN handling fixed
✅ Syntax errors corrected
✅ Notification queue bounded
✅ Auth errors differentiated from network errors

System now has:
✅ Comprehensive error handling coverage
✅ Detailed architecture documentation
✅ Clear patterns for future development
✅ Security best practices documented
✅ Observability strategy defined

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

commit.message:
fix: Open-Meteo 429 retry + OpenAI 400 logging and collage fallback

- Weather: reduce cache TTL to 30min, add retry with backoff on 429,
  fallback season by month (dec-mar=winter) when API unavailable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Track E: decodeFseLoop_probs_ge_neg1 — loop probability bound invariant (#935)

* feat: decodeFseLoop_probs_ge_neg1 — loop probability bound invariant

Proves that decodeFseLoop preserves the property that all probability
values in the output array have .toInt ≥ -1 when input probs satisfy
the same. This is the second deliverable for issue #913.

Key helper lemmas:
- push_preserves_ge_neg1: Array.push preserves element bounds
- pushZeros_probs_ge_neg1: pushZeros only adds zeros (≥ -1)
- decodeZeroRepeats_probs_ge_neg1: zero-repeat decoding preserves bounds
- readBit_value_lt_2: readBit returns 0 or 1
- readBits_go_value_lt_pow2: readBits accumulation stays < 2^n
- fwd_readBits_value_lt_pow2: forward readBits value bound
- readProbValue_le: readProbValue returns ≤ remaining
- int32_ofNat_sub_one_ge_neg1: Int32.ofNat n - 1 ≥ -1 when n < 2^31

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for FSE distribution bounds session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add Nat.shiftLeft_eq matching pattern to lean-simp-tactics skill

Documents the reliable `have hshift` pattern for converting `<<<` to
`2^k` when `simp only [Nat.shiftLeft_eq] at *` fails to match through
the HShiftLeft typeclass chain. Also documents the `generalize` pattern
for making `2^n` expressions linear for omega.

commit.message:
fix: production readiness hardening — 10 systemic risk fixes

1. DB pool: fleet.py asyncpg pool 10→50 max connections
2. Silent errors: replaced 28 bare except:pass with typed catches + logging
   across sites.py, evidence_chain.py, db_queries.py
3. Missing indexes: migration 073 adds 7 composite indexes for checkin perf
4. Agent cert renewal: CertNeedsRenewal() + RenewCerts() auto-renew at 30d
5. Agent reconnect: NewGRPCClient returns client on failure (prev commit)
6. Reconnect loop: now persistent, monitors for disconnection
7. GPO discovery: autodeploy.go dynamically finds domain GPO instead of
   hardcoding "Default Domain Policy"
8. Partner auth fallback: added deprecation warning for keyless checkin

All Go code builds clean. 199 Python tests pass.

commit.message:
feat: bundle premium Google Fonts for slideshow text rendering

Replace system fonts (FreeSans, Liberation, DejaVu) with bundled fonts
that match the visual quality expected for Instagram Reels/TikTok:
- bold:    Bebas Neue (condensed headline, the go-to reels font)
- elegant: Playfair Display (premium serif, replaces Liberation Serif)
- modern:  Montserrat Bold (clean app/tech content, replaces DejaVu Sans)
- minimal: Work Sans Light (airy and neutral, replaces FreeSans)
- kinetic: Anton (strong contrast, high energy, replaces Liberation Sans Bold)

detectFonts() now checks ./fonts/ first, then fontconfig, then apt paths.
System font packages kept in nixpacks.toml as fallback only.

fix: retry per chunk in multipart, reduce concurrency for large files

commit.message:
feat: use relative node-to-torch positions instead of absolute coordinates

Replace node features x/Lx, y/Ly, torch_x/Lx, torch_y/Ly (4 features)
with (node_x - torch_x)/Lx, (node_y - torch_y)/Ly (2 features).
node_in_dim: 7 → 5. Preserves translational invariance per MGN paper.

commit.message:
security(hardening): freeze data objects, sanitize search input, block fingerprinting APIs (v3.29.1)

Deep-freeze all exported data constants (VIDEOS, ARTIST_STATS, PORTFOLIO_STATS,
ALL_ARTISTS, NEON_COLORS) to prevent prototype pollution from third-party scripts
sharing the execution context. Add sanitizeSearchInput() stripping control chars,
NUL bytes, and zero-width Unicode before fuzzy matching. Extend Permissions-Policy
with browsing-topics, local-fonts, and window-management to block newer
fingerprinting vectors. 20 new security tests, 426 total passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add save state core compatibility tracking and warnings (#85)

Save states are not portable between different emulator cores. This change
adds cross-platform core compatibility awareness so users are warned when
a save state may be incompatible, and can choose how to proceed.

Server:
- Add coreMatch and currentCore fields to save state API responses
- Add GET /api/admin/core-compatibility endpoint showing per-console
  core differences between native and web platforms
- Track CoreName on SharedSession model
- New tests for core match logic, admin endpoint, and shared sessions

Web:
- Core mismatch warning modal on play page with three options:
  "Start with Game Save Only", "Try Loading Anyway", "Start Fresh"
- Warning indicators on save states in session detail view
- Admin core compatibility page showing native vs web core mismatches
- TypeScript types for new API fields

Player (KMP):
- Fix critical bug: SaveManager now sends coreName with all save uploads
  (was tracked locally but never sent to API)
- Core mismatch detection before auto-loading save states
- In-game CoreMismatchDialog showing core names and three options
- forceAutoLoadSaveState returns success/failure for proper UX feedback
- "Start Fresh" clears SRAM for a true fresh start

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
